speaker: LUCIUS
text: Approvèd warriors and my faithful friends,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I have receivèd letters from great Rome

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Which signifies what hate they bear their emperor

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And how desirous of our sight they are.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Therefore, great lords, be as your titles witness,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Imperious, and impatient of your wrongs,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And wherein Rome hath done you any scathe,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Let him make treble satisfaction.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Brave slip sprung from the great Andronicus,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Whose name was once our terror, now our comfort,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Whose high exploits and honorable deeds

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Ingrateful Rome requites with foul contempt,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Be bold in us. We’ll follow where thou lead’st,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Like stinging bees in hottest summer’s day

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: Led by their master to the flowered fields,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GOTH
text: And be avenged on cursèd Tamora.

speaker: GOTHS
text: And as he saith, so say we all with him.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I humbly thank him, and I thank you all.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: But who comes here, led by a lusty Goth?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Renownèd Lucius, from our troops I strayed

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: To gaze upon a ruinous monastery,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: And as I earnestly did fix mine eye

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Upon the wasted building, suddenly

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: I heard a child cry underneath a wall.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: I made unto the noise, when soon I heard

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: The crying babe controlled with this discourse:

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: “Peace, tawny slave, half me and half thy dame!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Did not thy hue bewray whose brat thou art,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Had nature lent thee but thy mother’s look,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Villain, thou mightst have been an emperor.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: But where the bull and cow are both milk white,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: They never do beget a coal-black calf.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Peace, villain, peace!”—even thus he rates the babe—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: “For I must bear thee to a trusty Goth

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Who, when he knows thou art the Empress’ babe,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Will hold thee dearly for thy mother’s sake.”

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: With this, my weapon drawn, I rushed upon him,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: Surprised him suddenly, and brought him hither

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GOTH
text: To use as you think needful of the man.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: O worthy Goth, this is the incarnate devil

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That robbed Andronicus of his good hand;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: This is the pearl that pleased your empress’ eye;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And here’s the base fruit of her burning lust.—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Say, wall-eyed slave, whither wouldst thou convey

speaker: LUCIUS
text: This growing image of thy fiendlike face?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Why dost not speak? What, deaf? Not a word?—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: A halter, soldiers! Hang him on this tree,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And by his side his fruit of bastardy.

speaker: AARON
text: Touch not the boy. He is of royal blood.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Too like the sire for ever being good.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: First hang the child, that he may see it sprawl,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: A sight to vex the father’s soul withal.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Get me a ladder.

speaker: AARON
text: Lucius, save the child

speaker: AARON
text: And bear it from me to the Empress.

speaker: AARON
text: If thou do this, I’ll show thee wondrous things

speaker: AARON
text: That highly may advantage thee to hear.

speaker: AARON
text: If thou wilt not, befall what may befall,

speaker: AARON
text: I’ll speak no more but “Vengeance rot you all!”

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Say on, and if it please me which thou speak’st,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thy child shall live, and I will see it nourished.

speaker: AARON
text: And if it please thee? Why, assure thee, Lucius,

speaker: AARON
text: ’Twill vex thy soul to hear what I shall speak;

speaker: AARON
text: For I must talk of murders, rapes, and massacres,

speaker: AARON
text: Acts of black night, abominable deeds,

speaker: AARON
text: Complots of mischief, treason, villainies,

speaker: AARON
text: Ruthful to hear, yet piteously performed.

speaker: AARON
text: And this shall all be buried in my death,

speaker: AARON
text: Unless thou swear to me my child shall live.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Tell on thy mind. I say thy child shall live.

speaker: AARON
text: Swear that he shall, and then I will begin.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Who should I swear by? Thou believest no god.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That granted, how canst thou believe an oath?

speaker: AARON
text: What if I do not? As indeed I do not.

speaker: AARON
text: Yet, for I know thou art religious

speaker: AARON
text: And hast a thing within thee callèd conscience,

speaker: AARON
text: With twenty popish tricks and ceremonies

speaker: AARON
text: Which I have seen thee careful to observe,

speaker: AARON
text: Therefore I urge thy oath; for that I know

speaker: AARON
text: An idiot holds his bauble for a god

speaker: AARON
text: And keeps the oath which by that god he swears,

speaker: AARON
text: To that I’ll urge him. Therefore thou shalt vow

speaker: AARON
text: By that same god, what god soe’er it be

speaker: AARON
text: That thou adorest and hast in reverence,

speaker: AARON
text: To save my boy, to nourish and bring him up,

speaker: AARON
text: Or else I will discover naught to thee.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Even by my god I swear to thee I will.

speaker: AARON
text: First know thou, I begot him on the Empress.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: O, most insatiate and luxurious woman!

speaker: AARON
text: Tut, Lucius, this was but a deed of charity

speaker: AARON
text: To that which thou shalt hear of me anon.

speaker: AARON
text: ’Twas her two sons that murdered Bassianus.

speaker: AARON
text: They cut thy sister’s tongue, and ravished her,

speaker: AARON
text: And cut her hands, and trimmed her as thou sawest.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: O detestable villain, call’st thou that trimming?

speaker: AARON
text: Why, she was washed, and cut, and trimmed; and ’twas

speaker: AARON
text: Trim sport for them which had the doing of it.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: O, barbarous beastly villains, like thyself!

speaker: AARON
text: Indeed, I was their tutor to instruct them.

speaker: AARON
text: That codding spirit had they from their mother,

speaker: AARON
text: As sure a card as ever won the set;

speaker: AARON
text: That bloody mind I think they learned of me,

speaker: AARON
text: As true a dog as ever fought at head.

speaker: AARON
text: Well, let my deeds be witness of my worth.

speaker: AARON
text: I trained thy brethren to that guileful hole

speaker: AARON
text: Where the dead corpse of Bassianus lay.

speaker: AARON
text: I wrote the letter that thy father found,

speaker: AARON
text: And hid the gold within that letter mentioned,

speaker: AARON
text: Confederate with the Queen and her two sons.

speaker: AARON
text: And what not done that thou hast cause to rue,

speaker: AARON
text: Wherein I had no stroke of mischief in it?

speaker: AARON
text: I played the cheater for thy father’s hand,

speaker: AARON
text: And, when I had it, drew myself apart

speaker: AARON
text: And almost broke my heart with extreme laughter.

speaker: AARON
text: I pried me through the crevice of a wall

speaker: AARON
text: When, for his hand, he had his two sons’ heads,

speaker: AARON
text: Beheld his tears, and laughed so heartily

speaker: AARON
text: That both mine eyes were rainy like to his.

speaker: AARON
text: And when I told the Empress of this sport,

speaker: AARON
text: She sounded almost at my pleasing tale,

speaker: AARON
text: And for my tidings gave me twenty kisses.

speaker: GOTH
text: What, canst thou say all this and never blush?

speaker: AARON
text: Ay, like a black dog, as the saying is.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Art thou not sorry for these heinous deeds?

speaker: AARON
text: Ay, that I had not done a thousand more.

speaker: AARON
text: Even now I curse the day—and yet, I think,

speaker: AARON
text: Few come within the compass of my curse—

speaker: AARON
text: Wherein I did not some notorious ill,

speaker: AARON
text: As kill a man, or else devise his death;

speaker: AARON
text: Ravish a maid or plot the way to do it;

speaker: AARON
text: Accuse some innocent and forswear myself;

speaker: AARON
text: Set deadly enmity between two friends;

speaker: AARON
text: Make poor men’s cattle break their necks;

speaker: AARON
text: Set fire on barns and haystalks in the night,

speaker: AARON
text: And bid the owners quench them with their tears.

speaker: AARON
text: Oft have I digged up dead men from their graves

speaker: AARON
text: And set them upright at their dear friends’ door,

speaker: AARON
text: Even when their sorrows almost was forgot,

speaker: AARON
text: And on their skins, as on the bark of trees,

speaker: AARON
text: Have with my knife carvèd in Roman letters

speaker: AARON
text: “Let not your sorrow die, though I am dead.”

speaker: AARON
text: But I have done a thousand dreadful things

speaker: AARON
text: As willingly as one would kill a fly,

speaker: AARON
text: And nothing grieves me heartily indeed

speaker: AARON
text: But that I cannot do ten thousand more.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Bring down the devil, for he must not die

speaker: LUCIUS
text: So sweet a death as hanging presently.

speaker: AARON
text: If there be devils, would I were a devil,

speaker: AARON
text: To live and burn in everlasting fire,

speaker: AARON
text: So I might have your company in hell

speaker: AARON
text: But to torment you with my bitter tongue.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sirs, stop his mouth, and let him speak no more.

speaker: GOTH
text: My lord, there is a messenger from Rome

speaker: GOTH
text: Desires to be admitted to your presence.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Let him come near.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Welcome, Aemilius. What’s the news from Rome?

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: Lord Lucius, and you princes of the Goths,

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: The Roman Emperor greets you all by me;

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: And, for he understands you are in arms,

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: He craves a parley at your father’s house,

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: Willing you to demand your hostages,

speaker: AEMILIUS
text: And they shall be immediately delivered.

speaker: GOTH
text: What says our general?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Aemilius, let the Emperor give his pledges

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Unto my father and my uncle Marcus,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And we will come. March away.
